meta.title: Voter Verification Guide
meta.description: Complete guide to adding members as voters, verifying identities, and managing election voter assignments with IP and device verification.

page.title: Voter Management Guide
page.subtitle: Learn how to assign members as voters and set up identity verification for your elections.

section_overview.heading: Overview
section_overview.body:
The Voter Management page allows election officers to assign eligible members as voters and set up identity verification to prevent fraud.

Key responsibilities:
• Add members to the election as eligible voters
• Configure verification modes (None, IP Only, Fingerprint Only, or Both)
• Conduct identity verification via video call
• Monitor voter status and manage suspensions

This guide walks through the entire process from assignment to verification.

section_prerequisites.heading: Before You Begin
section_prerequisites.intro: Ensure you have the following prerequisites in place:

steps.title: Election Created
steps.body: You have already created a real (non-demo) election. Demo elections do not support voter management.

steps.title: Officer Role
steps.body: You have been assigned the Election Officer role for this election by an Admin.

steps.title: Members Imported
steps.body: Organization members have been created and are marked as Active with fees paid or exempt.

steps.title: Membership Types (Optional)
steps.body: If using membership types, ensure they grant voting rights. Members without a type are eligible by default.

section_add_voters.heading: Adding Members as Voters

steps.title: Navigate to Voters Page
steps.body: Go to your election and click the Voters tab to see the voter management dashboard.

steps.title: Search for Members
steps.body: Use the search box to find eligible members by name or email. Only active members with paid/exempt fees appear.

steps.title: Select Members
steps.body: Click the checkbox next to one or more members. You can select up to 1,000 members at once.

steps.title: Click Assign Button
steps.body: Click the green Assign button. Selected members are instantly assigned as voters for this election.

steps.title: Verify Assignment
steps.body: Members now appear in the voters table. Their status is Pending until you approve them.

section_add_voters.eligibility_heading: Who Can Be Assigned as a Voter?
eligibility_items: Fees status must be Paid or Exempt
Membership type either: (a) none (eligible by default), OR (b) grants voting rights = true
Must not already be assigned to this election

section_verification_modes.heading: Voter Verification Modes
section_verification_modes.body: Elections support four verification modes to balance security with usability. You set the mode in Election Settings, and it applies to all voters in that election.

modes.name: None
modes.description: No verification required. Voters can vote immediately upon login.
modes.use_case: Small, low-risk organizations (sports clubs, hobby groups) where voter identity is already well-known.

modes.name: IP Only
modes.description: Voter must vote from a verified IP address. Prevents voting from multiple locations.
modes.use_case: Remote organizations where voters work from home offices. Officers verify once, voters can use that location for voting.

modes.name: Fingerprint Only
modes.description: Voter must use a verified device. Prevents voting from shared or borrowed devices.
modes.use_case: High-security elections where device ownership is critical (board votes, leadership elections).

modes.name: Both (IP + Fingerprint)
modes.description: Voter must vote from verified IP AND verified device. Maximum security.
modes.use_case: Diaspora organizations or international elections requiring maximum fraud prevention.

section_video_call.heading: Video Call Verification Workflow
steps: Member is assigned as voter (status: Pending)
Configure verification mode in Election Settings (None/IP Only/Fingerprint/Both)
Tell voter: 'Please log in from the location/device you'll use for voting'
Voter logs in — their IP and device fingerprint are automatically captured
Schedule a brief video call with the voter (over Zoom, phone, or in-person)
During call: open the Voter Management page and click the Verify button next to the voter
The Verify Modal opens with voter's IP pre-populated (read-only) and device fingerprint (read-only)
Confirm voter identity visually during the video call
Add optional notes about the verification (e.g., 'Verified passport', 'Video call 2024-01-15')
Click Verify Voter button in the modal
A green verification badge appears next to the voter's name — they can now vote!

section_ip_capture.heading: How IP Capture Works
section_ip_capture.body:
IP addresses are automatically captured when voters log in. You do NOT need to ask voters for their IP or manually enter it.

Here's why this matters:
1. Voters control their IP by choosing WHERE to log in (home, office, cafe, etc.)
2. Officers cannot edit or falsify the IP in the verification modal
3. The system enforces that voters vote from the same IP they logged in with
4. If a voter's IP changes (e.g., they move locations), they must re-verify
how_it_works: Voter logs in from their home office (IP: 192.168.1.100)
System captures IP automatically and stores it in their account
Officer verifies voter via video call — IP is read-only in the modal
Voter later votes — system confirms IP matches (192.168.1.100)
If voter tries voting from different IP (coffee shop), vote is blocked

modes.name: Mode: None
modes.behavior: Voter can vote immediately upon login. No verification check required.

modes.name: Mode: IP Only
modes.behavior: Voter must have an active IP verification. If unverified or IP mismatch, vote is denied with message: 'Your current IP does not match your verified IP.'

modes.name: Mode: Fingerprint Only
modes.behavior: Voter must have an active device verification. If unverified or device mismatch, vote is denied with message: 'Your device does not match your verified device.'

modes.name: Mode: Both (IP + Fingerprint)
modes.behavior: Voter must have BOTH active verifications and both must match. Any mismatch blocks vote: 'IP or device mismatch. Cannot vote from this location/device.'

items.title: Scenario 1: Small Club Election (No Verification)
items.setup: A sports club with 50 members votes on leadership positions. All members know each other; fraud risk is low.
steps: Set election verification mode to: None
Assign all 50 members as voters
No verification step needed — members can vote immediately upon login

items.title: Scenario 2: Remote Team Board Vote (IP Only)
items.setup: A remote-first software company (50 employees across 10 countries) votes on executive positions. Team members work from home offices.
steps: Set election verification mode to: IP Only
Tell team: 'Vote from your usual home office location'
Schedule brief video calls with each team member
During call, verify their identity and record their IP
Team members can vote anytime from that home office IP

items.title: Scenario 3: International Diaspora Organization (Both IP + Fingerprint)
items.setup: A 5,000-member diaspora organization with chapters in 12 countries. Members are scattered; fraud risk is very high.
steps: Set election verification mode to: Both
Schedule mandatory in-person or video verification sessions
During sessions: verify identity (passport/ID), record IP + device fingerprint
Members can only vote from verified location/device combination
Maximum security ensures one-person-one-vote guarantee

items.question: Can I assign a member who isn't in our organization?
items.answer: No. Only active organization members with paid/exempt fees can be assigned as voters. This is enforced at the system level.

items.question: What if a voter's IP changes after I verify them?
items.answer: If a voter logs in from a different IP, they will not be able to vote (if IP mode is enabled). They must contact you to re-verify with their new IP. You can open the Verify Modal again and confirm their new IP.

items.question: Can I edit the IP after verification?
items.answer: No. The IP field in the Verify Modal is read-only — it shows the voter's actual login IP and cannot be changed. This prevents fraudulent edits.

items.question: What if a member has no membership type?
items.answer: Members with NULL membership_type_id are eligible by default. They don't need any special membership type to vote.

items.question: Can I remove a voter after assigning them?
items.answer: Yes. Click the Remove button next to any voter to unassign them. If they haven't voted yet, they're immediately removed. If they've voted, the vote remains valid but they're marked removed.

items.question: What is the suspension system?
items.answer: Election Officers can propose suspending an active voter (e.g., for fraud). A second officer must confirm before suspension takes effect. This two-person approval prevents accidental vote suppression. Suspended voters cannot vote.

items.question: Can I export the voter list?
items.answer: Yes. Click the Export CSV button to download a list of all voters, their status, and assignment date. Useful for records and auditing.

items.question: What does the verification badge mean?
items.answer: The green checkmark badge next to a voter's name means their identity has been verified via video call (or equivalent) by an officer. They can now vote (if verification mode requires it).
